title: drift-classification/v1 — one classification row
description: The OUTPUT CONTRACT for the (future) LLM drift classifier. A classification is a schema-validated ROW, not prose. The classifier earns exactly ONE hop — classifying an ALREADY-DETECTED diff's materiality + contract owner — and every row it emits must pass scripts/classifier-walls.py before any side effect: this schema (output contract), the literal-substring pre-wall against the tier-2 vendored snapshot the diff came from, and the autonomy gradient in autonomy-policy.json. Schema identifier only — NOT an in-toto attestation predicate URI. Normative spec: 000-docs/055-AT-SPEC-llm-classifier-deterministic-walls-2026-06-12.md.

row_version.description: Optional self-describing version discriminator, matching the repo's registry_version/projection_version convention.

case_ref.description: Reference to an eval case (evals/drift-classification/v1 case id). A row carries case_ref (eval mode) or surface_id (live mode) — at least one is required.

surface_id.description: A `name` from specs/upstream-surface-registry.v1.json — the monitored surface whose tier-2 vendored snapshot the diff came from.

diff_ref.description: Reference to the ALREADY-DETECTED diff being classified (e.g. a spec-projection-diff finding, a tier-1 raw-record path, or a drift-issue ref). The classifier never detects drift itself — detection is deterministic and upstream of this row.

label.description: Materiality verdict. `material` = the diff plausibly changes a normative contract; `immaterial` = cosmetic/editorial.

contract_owner.description: Which contract owns the change — a `contract` value from specs/upstream-surface-registry.v1.json (e.g. skill-frontmatter, mcp-config, hook-config).

claimed_changed_fields.description: Every field the classification claims changed. PRE-WALL: each entry must appear as a LITERAL SUBSTRING of the fetched snapshot bytes the diff came from — else the row is rejected outright.

confidence.description: Classifier self-reported confidence in [0, 1]. Advisory — confidence never overrides a wall.

model_pin.description: Exact pinned model identifier that produced the classification (no aliases like `latest`).

then.description: A material claim with zero claimed fields would dodge the substring pre-wall entirely — forbidden by construction.
